title: Sparkwright Config
description: User-editable settings shared by the CLI and the interactive TUI. Loaded (in order, later overriding earlier) from ~/.config/sparkwright/config.json, <workspace>/.sparkwright/config.json, and $SPARKWRIGHT_CONFIG. CLI args and env vars override files. model/providers/permissionMode/workspace apply to both surfaces; theme/keybindings/mouse are TUI-only. The `providers` map is merged by key, `capabilities` merges by sub-capability, and `shell.sandbox` merges conservatively so later layers cannot weaken earlier sandbox policy; other shared fields are wholesale-overridden.

$schema.description: Optional reference to this JSON Schema so editors can offer completion and validation. Ignored by the loader.

model.description: Active model in the form "provider/model", e.g. "openai/gpt-example". The provider segment names an entry in `providers`. The reserved provider "deterministic" selects the built-in offline demo model and needs no `providers` entry.

providers.description: Named model providers. The key (e.g. "openai", "local") is referenced by `model`. The reserved name "deterministic" is built in and must not be declared here.

npm.description: AI SDK package used to construct the client. Defaults to "@ai-sdk/openai". Only "@ai-sdk/openai" is wired today (covers any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via baseURL); other values are reserved for future use and rejected at runtime.

baseURL.description: OpenAI-compatible base URL. Overridden by the OPENAI_BASE_URL env var when set.

apiKey.description: API key for this provider. Overridden by the OPENAI_API_KEY env var when set. Stored in plaintext — keep this file private (chmod 600).

models.description: Optional per-model metadata. Models not listed here still work; the metadata only attaches cost reporting.

cost.description: Per-million-token pricing used to attach costUsd to usage.

permissionMode.description: Permission policy mode for runs started from the TUI.

workspace.description: Workspace root for runs. Relative paths resolve from the directory of the config file that defined them.

confidentialPaths.description: Opt-in read-confidentiality. Workspace-relative paths or globs whose contents a run must not read; matching read_file/grep reads are denied at the tool layer. Supports '*' (single segment), '**' (any depth), and a bare directory name to cover everything beneath it.

shell.description: Host shell execution boundary. The safety classifier and approval policy still run before shell execution; this config controls the OS-level sandbox used by the host shell executor.

sandbox.description: OS-level process sandbox for local command execution. Forced deny-write paths such as Sparkwright config files are always appended by the host and cannot be removed by config. Linux uses bind-based allowlist isolation; macOS currently uses deny-list/network guardrails rather than complete filesystem hiding.

mode.description: off disables OS sandboxing; warn uses it when available and falls back with metadata when unavailable; enforce fails shell execution when sandboxing is unavailable.

failIfUnavailable.description: Fail closed when the platform sandbox runtime is unavailable. `mode: enforce` implies this.

capabilities.description: Host-owned capability runtime settings. These settings decide what the host may prepare before createRun(); the TUI may inspect the resulting host facts but must not load capabilities itself.

tools.description: Tool availability settings for host-created runs. These settings only affect which tools the host prepares before createRun(); execution still goes through policy and approval.

enabled.description: Optional allowlist of tool-name patterns. Omit to allow all host-assembled tools before other filters apply. Supports '*' as a wildcard.

disabled.description: Tool-name patterns removed from the prepared run tool set. Supports '*' as a wildcard.

defer.description: Tool-name patterns prepared as deferred schemas when the tool does not force eager loading. Supports '*' as a wildcard.

hooks.description: Deterministic workflow hooks for host-created runs. Core owns lifecycle execution; the host interprets configured actions.

skills.description: Skill loading settings for host-created runs.

roots.description: Skill root directories. Relative paths resolve from the config file that defined them.

includeLoaderTool.description: Register the progressive skill_load tool so the model can load Skill bodies on demand. The host defaults this to true.

loadSelectedSkills.description: Load matcher-selected Skill bodies into resident run context up front. The host defaults this to false (on-demand loading via skill_load); set true for auto-resident bodies.

maxSelectedSkills.description: Maximum matched Skill bodies to load for one run.

resourceFileLimit.description: Maximum Skill resource files exposed by the skill_load tool.

mcp.description: MCP server settings for host-created runs.

agents.description: Agent profile run templates for host-created runs. Profiles define role guidance, tool boundaries, policy, and budget; callable delegation tools must be declared explicitly.

delegateTools.description: Explicit child-profile delegation tools exposed to the parent model. Omit to keep profiles inspectable only.

theme.description: Visual theme. 'dark' (default) uses bold ANSI accents; 'light' adapts for light terminals; 'mono' is grayscale-only.

mouse.description: Enable mouse reporting so the scroll wheel scrolls the event log. Default true. Disable if it conflicts with your terminal's text selection.

keybindings.description: Override default key chords for named TUI actions. Set to '' or [] to clear a default (unbind). Chord syntax: 'ctrl+k', 'shift+tab', '?', 'esc'. Known names: palette.open, help.open, cancel.run, quit.app.
